title: ALP Build Plan v1
description: The machine-readable build plan `scripts/alp_orchestrate/buildplan.py::emit_build_plan` renders for a resolved board.yaml project (the `alp` CLI / alp-sdk-vscode 'Wave C' consumer contract, settled 2026-06-04). One entry per non-`off` core slice, each carrying its build command, config artefact contents, and app source dir, plus the shared (cross-slice) generated artefacts and any non-fatal planning warnings. Every artefact carries its full `contents` so the consumer's materialise step stays pure IO -- the plan and the Orchestrator's own on-disk materialise step are written from the same `_shared_artefacts`/`_slice_config_artefact` helpers and MUST agree byte-for-byte. camelCase keys (this is a CLI-consumer contract, not an in-repo YAML). Stability: schemaVersion 1 is locked with the alp-sdk-vscode consumer -- bump schemaVersion and record the change in the CHANGELOG before altering this shape; do not add breaking changes silently.

schemaVersion.description: Build-plan contract version. Independent of board.yaml's own schema_version.

generatedBy.description: Identifies the emitting tool (currently the literal 'scripts/alp_orchestrate.py'). Informational only -- not part of the locked consumer contract.

boardYaml.description: POSIX-style path to the board.yaml this plan was resolved from, as passed to emit_build_plan (issue #596: every relative path elsewhere in the plan is anchored on this file's own directory, never the emitting process's CWD).

buildRoot.description: POSIX-style path to the root build directory every slice's buildDir and the shared artefacts are nested under.

slices.description: One entry per core whose resolved os is not `off`, sorted by coreId. A core this emitter cannot yet produce a build command for (e.g. missing app/board/image, or an app-only Yocto slice with no recipe:) is still carried here with command: null plus a matching entry in `warnings` -- never silently dropped.

backend.description: The slice's resolved os (Slice.os). `off` cores never reach the plan, so this enum excludes it.

appDir.description: Resolved absolute (POSIX-style) path to this slice's app source directory, independent of `command` -- lets tooling watch the app source without reverse-engineering it out of a yocto/zephyr/baremetal-shaped command (issue #597). null when the slice has no app source dir to report, e.g. a Yocto slice built from the `alp-image-edge` stock-image token rather than an `app:` path.

configArtefacts.description: This slice's own generated config artefact(s) (alp.conf / local.conf / cmake-args.txt), or an empty array when the slice's os has none. Byte-identical to what the Orchestrator's own materialise step writes to buildDir.

path.description: POSIX-style path (under buildDir) the consumer should write `contents` to.

contents.description: Full file contents, ready to byte-write verbatim.

command.description: The build command to run for this slice, or null when this emitter has no valid command yet (see `slices` description; a matching `warnings` entry explains why).

cwd.description: POSIX-style working directory to run `tool` from; always equal to this slice's buildDir.

env.description: Environment variables the consumer must inject into the slice subprocess, native host-path form (handed to the subprocess environment verbatim).

ALP_SDK_ROOT.description: Absolute host path to the alp-sdk checkout the plan was generated from.

sharedArtefacts.description: Cross-slice generated artefacts (e.g. generated/alp/system_ipc.h, dts-reservations.dtsi, dts-partitions.dtsi, and -- only when the project declares them -- alp_sysbuild.conf / sysbuild/tfm/tfm.conf). Conditional artefacts follow absence-emits-nothing: they appear only when their emit is non-empty. Byte-identical to what the Orchestrator's own materialise step writes under buildRoot.

path.description: POSIX-style path (under buildRoot) the consumer should write `contents` to.

contents.description: Full file contents, ready to byte-write verbatim.

warnings.description: Non-fatal planning warnings, one per affected core, machine-readable (`code`) plus a human-readable `message` so the consumer can surface -- or filter -- them without string-matching prose.

code.description: Stable machine-readable warning code. Known values as of schemaVersion 1: 'no-command' (missing app/board/image for the core's os), 'yocto-recipe-missing' (an app-only Yocto slice has app: but no recipe:, so there is no valid bitbake target). New codes may be added without a schemaVersion bump -- consumers must not treat this as a closed enum.

coreId.description: The core this warning applies to (matches a `slices[].coreId`).

message.description: Human-readable explanation, safe to display verbatim.
